title: Restoring an item from the Recycle Bin via API
excerpt: Is it possible to restore an item from the Recycle Bin to it’s board via API/GraphQL? If so, what would that query look like?

posts.author: dougflowersNCSU (Doug Flowers)
posts.cookedHtml: Is it possible to restore an item from the Recycle Bin to it’s board via API/GraphQL? If so, what would that query look like?

posts.author: basdebruin (Bas de Bruin)
posts.cookedHtml:
hi @dougflowersNCSU

Welcome to the community. There is no possibility to restore an item trough the API. You can query the item to see if the item is archived or deleted, but you can’t unarchive or undelete it. A possible alternative is to duplicate the archived / deleted item. I have not tested this but I think it will create a new item (not deleted / archived) into the board you select.

posts.author: AlexSavchuk (Alex Savchuk)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey @dougflowersNCSU

That’s a great question! I can only confirm @basdebruin 's great response here.

Currently, this is not possible, so I’m glad to move this along to our feature suggestions section so other users can add their voice as well.

I’ve also passed this along to our developers, but at this time, I can’t provide any kind of ETA for when this would be an option.

I hope this helps!

-Alex

posts.author: MSIlydia (Lydia Taylor)
posts.cookedHtml: I have lots of different projects with lots of different associated tasks on different boards. Being able to un-archive an item via API would be huge, because if an old project got revived, for example, I could automatically un-archive all the old associated tasks. This would be helpful.

posts.author: Matias.Monday (Matias)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello @MSIlydia,

I have added your vote towards this request

Cheers,
Matias

posts.author: Matias.Monday (Matias)
posts.cookedHtml: Request monday.com: Where Teams Get Work Done

posts.author: CJandCJ (Caleb)
posts.cookedHtml: It would appear you can inconsistently/temporarily bring an item from a deleted/archived state to an active state by using the api to move the item to any active group other than the one it’s in. I’m getting mixed results. Sometimes the item appears but isn’t actually there ie. it’s still in a deleted state but you can visually see it on the board. And other times it goes into an active state and stays that way for at least some amount of time. Very weird. I’m posting this in hopes someone can figure out a workaround to make the items permanently undeleted/unarchived via API.

posts.author: Matias.Monday (Matias)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello @EyalSeagull,

I have added your vote!

Cheers,
Matias

posts.author: EyalSeagull (Eyal Seagull)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @Matias.Monday , thanks!

I could use the built-in webhook’s Delete Item integration, however, the event payload that I receive does not contain any token to access Monday (unlike an automation that has a shortLivedToken I can use).

Any suggestion? Thanks.

PS: In fact, not having a shortLivedToken in webhook event payloads restricts greatly the use cases for using the built-in webhook. I would like to add this as a request to Monday, please.

posts.author: basdebruin (Bas de Bruin)
posts.cookedHtml:
hi @EyalSeagull

The post to the webhookUrl is not signed and therefore it would be very unsecure to send a shortLivedToken with it. From the app that is receiving the webhook you can post back to the monday subsctiptionUrl and if that subscription is using a custom action the action will post back to your app with a shortLivedToken (because the request is signed)

posts.author: Matias.Monday (Matias)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello @EyalSeagull

Regular webhooks do not require any authorization by the users. There is no scopes authorization screen. That is why you don’t get any tokens.

For recipes that come from apps, there is a scopes authorization screen with the scopes that are required by the app to function. We only provide tokens to access or update information when scopes are authorized.

I will add your request to add the option to add a scopes authorization screen to regular webhooks in order to get tokens to access/update data.

Cheers,
Matias

posts.author: Matias.Monday (Matias)
posts.cookedHtml: Request monday.com: Where Teams Get Work Done

posts.author: EyalSeagull (Eyal Seagull)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @basdebruin,

I tried to follow your logic, but I’m afraid with no success. Is there a more detailed example / instructions you can give to follow?

Thanks for replying!
